scenario.title: Battery degradation claim just before plan expiration
scenario.description: Customer submits a claim for battery replacement due to capacity depletion measured at 49% just three days before the AppleCare+ plan expires.

service_fee.notes: Battery service under section 3.1 is free if capacity is 50% or more depleted; here it is just below threshold.

policy_references.snippet: Apple will repair or replace the battery if its capacity has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from original specifications.
Coverage ends twenty-four (24) months from the date of retail purchase of your original iPhone.
The Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or normal aging of the product.

scenario.reasoning: The claim is borderline because the battery capacity depletion is just under the 50% threshold required for free replacement under section 3.1. The plan is also nearing expiration, which may affect timing of service. The policy excludes normal wear and tear defects (4.2(vii)), and battery degradation near 50% may be considered normal aging. Apple may require additional diagnostic data or consider a margin of error in measurement to determine eligibility.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private not-for-profit, tax-exempt, or charitable organization
persona.detailed job description: Provides primary and specialty nursing care
persona.family presence and age: With related children under 5 years only
persona.household type: Married couple household with children of the householder less than 18
persona.industry category: MED-General Medical And Surgical Hospitals, And Specialty (Except Psychiatric And Substance Abuse) Hospitals
persona.mannerisms: Often uses hand gestures when speaking
persona.personal time: Spends free time with family or volunteering at local community center

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer submits a claim for battery replacement due to capacity depletion measured at 49% just three days before the AppleCare+ plan expires.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi there! I need to file a claim for a battery replacement on my iPhone 14 Pro Max. The battery capacity has dropped to 49%, and my AppleCare+ plan is about to expire in three days. Can you help me get this sorted out quickly?
messages.raw_text: Hi there! I need to file a claim for a battery replacement on my iPhone 14 Pro Max. The battery capacity has dropped to 49%, and my AppleCare+ plan is about to expire in three days. Can you help me get this sorted out quickly?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly! To get started, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's serial number? This will help me verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: Certainly! To get started, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's serial number? This will help me verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-7593842105, and the serial number of my iPhone is F2N7K8M3P1Q4. Let me know if you need anything else!
messages.raw_text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-7593842105, and the serial number of my iPhone is F2N7K8M3P1Q4. Let me know if you need anything else!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for that information. Could you please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone's battery in your own words? This helps us understand the problem better.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for that information. Could you please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone's battery in your own words? This helps us understand the problem better.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Absolutely. The battery is draining way too fast and the maximum capacity reading shows just 49%, which is way below what it should be. It’s really affecting my day-to-day use, especially with my busy schedule and family demands. I'd like to get it replaced before the plan ends.
messages.raw_text: Absolutely. The battery is draining way too fast and the maximum capacity reading shows just 49%, which is way below what it should be. It’s really affecting my day-to-day use, especially with my busy schedule and family demands. I'd like to get it replaced before the plan ends.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details. To confirm, what iOS version is your iPhone currently running? Also, have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the phone or resetting settings? Are there any peripherals connected when you notice the issue?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details. To confirm, what iOS version is your iPhone currently running? Also, have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the phone or resetting settings? Are there any peripherals connected when you notice the issue?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: My iPhone is running iOS 17.0.1. I have tried restarting it a couple of times, but the battery capacity issue remains. I haven’t reset any settings yet since I’m hoping to get the battery replaced under the plan. No peripherals are usually connected when I notice the battery drain. Just the phone itself.
messages.raw_text: My iPhone is running iOS 17.0.1. I have tried restarting it a couple of times, but the battery capacity issue remains. I haven’t reset any settings yet since I’m hoping to get the battery replaced under the plan. No peripherals are usually connected when I notice the battery drain. Just the phone itself.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text:
Thank you for the information. Since this is a battery-related issue and your battery capacity has dropped below 50%, we have what we need to proceed with your claim. I will now process your claim for the battery replacement. 

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text:
Thank you for the information. Since this is a battery-related issue and your battery capacity has dropped below 50%, we have what we need to proceed with your claim. I will now process your claim for the battery replacement. 

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thanks so much! I really appreciate how straightforward this is. No, that’s all for now. Just glad to get this resolved before the plan expires. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Thanks so much! I really appreciate how straightforward this is. No, that’s all for now. Just glad to get this resolved before the plan expires. Have a great day! <END>
